summary: Feb 4, 2026 redeploy verification of Vibe 654 (Solo + Coliseum + Tournament + Social), Card-MP (Euchre/Pinochle), HttpMultiplayer unification, and shield. Backend pytest 14/14 PASS for tests/test_card_multiplayer.py + tests/test_vibe_654_social.py. Backend regression shield 34/34 PASS (run_shield.sh --fast = green). Frontend: demo login + /vibe-654/solo render + /games/vibe654/tournament lobby + lobby-enter-solo navigation all WORK. TWO CRITICAL FRONTEND ISSUES FOUND: (a) Solo Vault Ante-In flow is BROKEN — POST /api/vibez-654/start returns HTTP 402, decision PopDown / Roll / Stand never render, blocking the entire Solo loop; (b) HttpMultiplayer unification redirects are NOT WIRED — /http-multiplayer/hearts, /http-multiplayer/rummy, /http-multiplayer/gin_rummy all bounce to / (homepage) instead of the AAA canonical pages. Plus a MEDIUM testid gap: stake preset buttons (500/1k/5k/25k/100k/500k) have no data-testid, so vibe654-stake-1000 etc cannot be selected programmatically. NOT SAFE TO REDEPLOY until 402-on-start and HttpMultiplayer redirects are fixed.

critical.issue: Returns HTTP 402 Payment-Required when authenticated demo user (token + cookie + 99,990₵ wallet) attempts to ante-in at the 1000₵ stake on /vibe-654/solo. UI surfaces the raw text 'POST /api/vibez-654/start 402' and never reveals the dice tray nor the vibe654-decision-popdown. This single failure blocks the entire Solo Vault loop the redeploy is meant to ship. Likely root cause: demo user's stake-validation in vibez_654 router does not look up credits via the same field the wallet UI reads (credits_balance) OR a min-stake/age-gate check is rejecting the demo session. Repro: demo-login → /vibe-654/solo → click 1k stake → click Ante In → 402.

ui_bugs.issue: HttpMultiplayer unification redirects are NOT wired. Visiting /http-multiplayer/hearts, /http-multiplayer/rummy, /http-multiplayer/gin_rummy each lands the user on '/' (homepage) — NOT on /hearts, /rummy, /gin-rummy as the spec requires. Verified via direct page.goto on the public preview URL. Either the redirect declarations were not committed to /app/frontend/src/routes/gamesRoutes.tsx, or the legacy HttpGameRouter still owns these paths and isn't issuing a <Navigate to=... replace />. Affects all three routes equally → the unification PR is effectively a no-op in the deployed build.

Stake preset buttons (₵500 / ₵1k / ₵5k / ₵25k / ₵100k / ₵500k) have NO data-testid attributes. Spec requires data-testid='vibe654-stake-1000' (and 500/5000/25000/100000/500000) so QA can drive the stake choice deterministically. Workaround possible (text match) but spec parity fails.

Privy SDK keeps throwing 'TypeError: e is not a function' + CSP frame-ancestors 403 + a flood of postMessage warnings on EVERY page load (not only /login) because PrivyProvider mounts globally. Self-hide guard still does not silence it. Not a regression but pollutes console aggressively on every preview-domain visit.

integration_issues.issue: Click 'Ante In' → backend POST /api/vibez-654/start → 402 Payment-Required → UI shows error string under the Ante button and the Coliseum stays in the 'ANTE IN · NOVA IS READY' idle state forever. No dice render, no decision popdown, no roll/stand buttons. End-to-end Solo loop is unusable.

action_items: (CRITICAL) Fix POST /api/vibez-654/start 402 for the demo session at the 1000₵ stake on /vibe-654/solo. Investigate the start handler's wallet/age/min-stake gate. Repro: demo-login → /vibe-654/solo → 1k → Ante In → 402. Without this fix, the Solo Vault feature in this PR is non-functional.

(HIGH) Wire HttpMultiplayer → AAA redirects. /http-multiplayer/hearts must Navigate to /hearts (replace), /http-multiplayer/rummy → /rummy, /http-multiplayer/gin_rummy → /gin-rummy. Confirm the redirect <Route> entries are placed BEFORE any catch-all /http-multiplayer/* fallback in gamesRoutes.tsx. Right now all three land on /.

(MEDIUM) Add data-testid attributes to the six stake preset buttons in VibeSoloHighRoller.tsx: vibe654-stake-500, vibe654-stake-1000, vibe654-stake-5000, vibe654-stake-25000, vibe654-stake-100000, vibe654-stake-500000.
(LOW carry-over) Tighten PrivyLoginButton self-hide on the preview domain — it pollutes every page's console with TypeError + CSP 403 + postMessage warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend pytest is rock-solid: 14/14 (7 card-mp + 7 social) + 34/34 shield = 48 pytests green. The 402 we saw on /api/vibez-654/start is NOT covered by these suites — there is no public-URL pytest for the Solo Vault start flow. ADD ONE so this exact regression cannot ship next time.

/api/card-multiplayer/* prefix (backend) vs /card-mp/:gameType/:roomId (frontend page route) is intentional and consistent — frontend correctly calls /api/card-multiplayer/{create-room, rooms, room/{id}/join, fill-bots, start, bid, play-card, next-hand}. No confusion there.
vibe654-coliseum-solo + vibe654-solo-start + vibe654-lobby-enter-solo testids are all present. The lobby → solo navigation works.

HttpMultiplayer unification claim in the PR description is unverified by deployed build — pages still resolve to / instead of the AAA pages. Either gamesRoutes.tsx changes weren't included or they're below a wildcard route.

success_rate.backend: 100% pytest (48/48 in scope) but 1 untested public endpoint failing in browser (/api/vibez-654/start 402 → call it 95%)
success_rate.frontend: ~55% — page renders + login + lobby OK; Solo Ante flow broken; 3 redirect routes broken

test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (no body). Demo email demo@globalvibez.com, returns Bearer token saved to localStorage.auth_token + session_token cookie. Wallet auto-seeded with 99,990₵ from prior iteration.
seed_data_creation: None. Created one ephemeral Vibe 654 tournament table (₵100,000 buy-in) during exploratory check — left in place since it does not interfere with the Solo or MP suites.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PRIORITY 1: confirm POST /api/vibez-654/start no longer returns 402 for demo session at 1000₵ stake. Curl repro: TOK=$(curl -sX POST $URL/api/auth/demo-login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' | jq -r .token); curl -X POST $URL/api/vibez-654/start -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"stake":1000}'. PRIORITY 2: confirm /http-multiplayer/hearts → /hearts (and rummy + gin_rummy variants). PRIORITY 3: re-run the in-browser Solo loop (Ante → Roll → Stand → Score) once the 402 is gone, then test Euchre + Pinochle MP modal → Create → CardMpRoomPage → Fill Bots → Start → phase=='bidding' (not retested this round because Solo critical bug took priority and token budget was tight). Existing pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_card_multiplayer.py + tests/test_vibe_654_social.py + iter_feb4_vibe654_cardmp.xml are all green and can be reused as-is.
rca of the issue: (1) /api/vibez-654/start 402: Solo Vault start handler returns Payment-Required even though demo user has 99,990₵ in credits_balance. Likely cause = either (a) the start endpoint reads a different wallet field (e.g. token_balance) than the wallet UI displays (credits_balance), or (b) an age/identity gate is firing because demo profile.dob is null, or (c) min-stake or daily-cap rule rejects 1000₵. Inspect /app/backend/routes/vibe_654_*.py around the POST /start handler. (2) HttpMultiplayer redirects: declared but not effective in deployed bundle — visits land on '/'. Check that /app/frontend/src/routes/gamesRoutes.tsx places the new <Route path='/http-multiplayer/hearts' element={<Navigate to='/hearts' replace />} /> entries BEFORE any wildcard /http-multiplayer/* catch-all in HttpGameRouter, and that the build actually included them (cleared build cache, rebuilt frontend bundle). Mitigation: ship both fixes together since they are independent; rerun this report's exact 4-step Playwright probe.
